= Harper Hans Hudson =

{{CharacterInfobox
| name        = Harper Hans Hudson
| portrait    = https://foundry.atkennedy.com/assets/Images/Tokens/hans%20token.png
| level       = 5
| size        = Medium
| hp          = 53/53
| ac          = 16
| perception  = +11
| speed       = 25 ft.
| fortitude   = +9
| reflex      = +9
| will        = +13
| attributes  = '''STR''' +4 | '''DEX''' +2 | '''CON''' +2 | '''INT''' +0 | '''WIS''' +4 | '''CHA''' +0
| key_ability  = STR
| hero_points  = 0
| languages   = dwarven
| wealth      = 1 PP, 16 GP, 4 SP, 4 CP
}}

== Details ==
* '''Ancestry:''' Human
* '''Heritage:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/environment/people/group.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Versatile Human" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Versatile Human
* '''Background:''' Field Medic
* '''Class:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/classes/cleric.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Cleric" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Cleric
* '''Key Ability:''' STR
* '''XP:''' 40
* '''Languages:''' dwarven

== Personality & Beliefs ==
; '''Edicts'''
: be humble

help others perfect themselves

hone your body, mind, and spirit to a more perfect state

practice discipline
; '''Anathema'''
: destroy an important historical text

engage in overly unhealthy or self-destructive behaviors

repeatedly fail to maintain self-control

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>⚔ Defense & Saves</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
* '''HP:''' 53/53
* '''AC:''' 16
* '''Perception:''' +11 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
* '''Speed:''' 25 ft. (land)
* '''Fortitude:''' +9 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
* '''Reflex:''' +9 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
* '''Will:''' +13 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>📊 Ability Scores</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; width:100%;"
|-
! STR !! DEX !! CON !! INT !! WIS !! CHA
|-
| +4 || +2 || +2 || +0 || +4 || +0
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎓 Skills</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="width:100%;"
! Skill !! Modifier !! Proficiency
|-
| Acrobatics || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Arcana || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Athletics || +13 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Crafting || +9 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Deception || +9 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Diplomacy || +7 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Intimidation || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Legal || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Medicine || +13 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Nature || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Occultism || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Performance || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Religion || +11 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Society || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Stealth || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Survival || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Thievery || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>💰 Wealth & Carry</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable"
! Coin !! Total Value !! Bulk !! Invested
|-
| 1 PP, 16 GP, 4 SP, 4 CP || 26.44 gp || 33.9 / 9 enc / 14 max || 0/10
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>✨ Feats & Features</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Heritage=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/environment/people/group.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Versatile Human" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Versatile Human'''
: Humanity's versatility and ambition have fueled its ascendance to be the most common ancestry in most nations throughout the world. Select a general feat of your choice for which you meet the prerequisites (as with your ancestry feat, you can select this general feat at any point during character creation).

=====Background Features=====
; '''Field Medic'''
: In the chaotic rush of battle, you learned to adapt to rapidly changing conditions as you administered to battle casualties. You patched up soldiers, guards, or other combatants, and learned a fair amount about the logistics of war.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to '''Constitution''' or '''Wisdom''', and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the Medicine skill and the Warfare Lore skill. You gain the Battle Medicine skill feat.

=====Feats=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Alkenstar Agent Dedication" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Alkenstar Agent Dedication''' <small>(archetype, dedication)</small>
: '''Access''' You have been initiated by the grand duchess of Alkenstar or one of her agents.

You put on the persona of a rough and tumble outlaw, but your real goals are to report threats to Alkenstar back to the grand duchess. You become an expert in Deception and trained in your choice of Underworld Lore or Legal Lore; if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. You gain the Lie to Me skill feat.

'''Special''' You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the Alkenstar Agent archetype.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Armor Proficiency (Heavy)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Armor Proficiency (Heavy)''' <small>(general)</small>
: You become trained in light armor. If you already were trained in light armor, you gain training in medium armor. If you were trained in both, you become trained in heavy armor. If you are at least 13th level, you become an expert in this armor type.

'''Special''' You can select this feat more than once. Each time, you become trained in the next type of armor, as detailed above.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Battle Medicine" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Battle Medicine''' <small>(general, healing, manipulate, skill)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You're holding or wearing a healer's toolkit.

You can patch up wounds, even in combat. Attempt a Medicine check with the same DC as for Treat Wounds and restore the corresponding amount of HP; this doesn't remove the wounded condition. As with Treat Wounds, you can attempt checks against higher DCs if you have the minimum proficiency rank. The target is then immune to your Battle Medicine for 1 day. This does not make them immune to, or otherwise count as, Treat Wounds.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/axes/axe-double-gold.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Cleric Spellcasting" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Cleric Spellcasting''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: Your deity bestows on you the power to cast divine spells. You are a spellcaster, and you can cast spells of the divine tradition using the Cast a Spell activity. As a cleric, your chants generally invoke your deity and their powerful servants by name or title, while your gestures are followed by sacred symbols or other representations of your deity.

At 1st level, you can prepare two 1st-rank spells and five cantrips each morning from the common spells on the divine spell list or from other divine spells to which you gain access and learn via Learn a Spell. Prepared spells remain available to you until you cast them or until you prepare your spells again. The number of spells you can prepare each day is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a cleric, the number of spells you can prepare each day increases, as does the highest rank of spell you can cast, as shown in Cleric Spells per Day table above.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack to see how effective they are or for your enemies to roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key attribute is Wisdom, your spell attack modifier and spell DC use your Wisdom modifier.

=== Heightening Spells ===

When you get spell slots of 2nd rank and higher, you can fill those slots with stronger versions of lower-rank spells. This increases the spell's rank, heightening it to match the spell slot. Many spells have specific improvements when they are heightened to certain ranks.

=== Cantrips ===

Some of your spells are cantrips. A cantrip is a special type of spell that doesn't use spell slots. You can cast a cantrip at will, any number of times per day. A cantrip is always automatically heightened to half your level rounded up—this is usually equal to the highest rank of cleric spell slot you have. For example, as a 1st-level cleric, your cantrips are 1st-rank spells, and as a 5th-level cleric, your cantrips are 3rd-rank spells.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Clever Improviser" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Clever Improviser''' <small>(human)</small>
: You've learned how to handle situations when you're out of your depth. You gain the Untrained Improvisation general feat. In addition, you can attempt skill actions that normally require you to be trained, even if you are untrained.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Continual Recovery" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Continual Recovery''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You zealously monitor a patient's progress to administer treatment faster. When you Treat Wounds, your patient becomes immune for only 10 minutes instead of 1 hour. This applies only to your Treat Wounds activities, not any other the patient receives.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Deadly Simplicity" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Deadly Simplicity''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: When you are wielding your deity's favored weapon, increase the damage die size of that weapon by one step.

If your deity's favored weapon is an unarmed attack (such as a fist, if you worship Irori) and its damage die is smaller than d6, instead increase its damage die size to d6.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/deity.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Deity (Cleric) (Irori)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Deity (Cleric) (Irori)''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: As a cleric, you are a mortal servitor of a deity you revere above all others. Your deity grants you the trained proficiency rank in one skill and with the deity's favored weapon. If the favored weapon is uncommon, you also get access to that weapon.

Your deity also adds spells to your spell list. You can prepare these just like you can any spell on the divine spell list once you can prepare spells of their rank as a cleric. Any of these spells that aren't normally on the divine list are still divine spells if you prepare them this way.

=== Sanctification ===

Depending on your deity, their sanctification can make you holy or unholy. This gives you the holy or unholy trait, which commits you to one side of a struggle over the souls of the planes and may be referenced in other abilities. If you "can be" holy or unholy according to your deity, you make that choice, and if you "must be" holy or unholy you gain the trait automatically. If you gain the opposing trait in some way, you lose the previous trait until you complete an atone ritual.

=== Anathema ===

Acts fundamentally opposed to your deity's ideals are anathema to your faith. Learning or casting spells, committing acts, and using items that are anathema to your deity remove you from your deity's good graces.

Casting spells with the unholy trait is almost always anathema to deities who don't allow unholy sanctification, and casting holy spells is likewise anathema to those who don't allow holy sanctification. Similarly, casting spells that are anathema to the tenets or goals of your faith could interfere with your connection to your deity. For example, casting a spell to create undead would be anathema to Pharasma, the goddess of death. Many actions that are anathema don't appear in any deity's formal list. For borderline cases, you and your GM determine which acts are anathema.

If you perform enough acts that are anathema to your deity, you lose the magical abilities that come from your connection to your deity. The class features that you lose are determined by the GM, but they likely include your divine font and all cleric spellcasting. These abilities can be regained only if you repent by conducting an atone ritual.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/fire/flame-burning-hand-white.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Divine Font (Healing)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Divine Font (Healing)''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: Through your deity's blessing, you gain additional spells that channel either the life force called vitality or its counterforce, the void. When you prepare your spells each day, you can prepare additional Heal or Harm spells, depending on your deity. The divine font spell your deity provides is listed in the Divine Font entry for your deity; if both are listed, you can choose between heal or harm. Once you choose, you can't change your choice short of divine intervention.

'''Healing Font:''' You gain 4 additional spell slots each day at your highest rank of cleric spell slots. You can prepare only heal spells in these slots. At 5th level, the number of additional slots increases to 5, and at 15th level, the total number of additional slots increases to 6.

'''Harmful Font:''' You gain 4 additional spell slots each day at your highest rank of cleric spell slots. You can prepare only harm spells in these slots. At 5th level, the number of additional slots increases to 5, and at 15th level, the total number of additional slots increases to 6.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/symbols/question-stone-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Doctrine" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Doctrine''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: Even among followers of the same deity, approaches vary. At 1st level, you select cloistered cleric or warpriest and gain the benefits of its first doctrine. At 3rd level and every four levels thereafter, you gain another benefit from your doctrine.

* Cloistered Cleric

* Warpriest
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Domain Initiate (Might)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Domain Initiate (Might)''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: Your deity bestows a special spell related to their powers. Select one domain—a subject of particular interest to you within your religion—from your deity's list. You gain an initial domain spell for that domain, a spell unique to the domain and not available to other clerics.

Domain spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to pray to your deity or do service toward their causes.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up, much like cantrips. Focus spells don't require spell slots, and can't be cast using spell slots. Your focus pool can hold one Focus Point for each focus spell you have, up to 3 points.

'''Special''' You can select this feat multiple times, selecting a different domain each time and gaining its domain spell.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Emblazon Armament" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Emblazon Armament''' <small>(cleric, exploration)</small>
: Carefully etching a sacred image into a physical object, you steel yourself for battle. You can spend 10 minutes emblazoning a symbol of your deity upon a weapon or shield. The symbol doesn't fade until 1 year has passed, but if you Emblazon an Armament, any symbol you previously emblazoned, and any symbol already emblazoned on that item instantly disappears.

The emblazoned item is a religious symbol of your deity in addition to its normal purpose, and it gains another benefit determined by the type of item. The benefit applies only to followers of the deity the symbol represents, but others can use the item normally.

* '''Shield''' The shield gains a +1 status bonus to its Hardness. (This causes it to reduce more damage with the Shield Block reaction.)

* '''Weapon''' The wielder gains a +1 status bonus to damage rolls with the weapon.

Effect: Emblazon Armament
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/symbols/question-stone-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="First Doctrine" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''First Doctrine''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: You gain the first benefit of your doctrine:

* Cloistered Cleric

* Warpriest
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-helmet-metal.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="First Doctrine (Warpriest)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''First Doctrine (Warpriest)''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: You're trained in light and medium armor, and you have expert proficiency in Fortitude saves. You gain the Shield Block general feat, a reaction to reduce damage with a shield. If your deity's favored weapon is a simple weapon or an unarmed attack, you gain the Deadly Simplicity cleric feat. At 13th level, if you gain the Divine Defense class feature, you also gain expert proficiency in light and medium armor.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fleet" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Fleet''' <small>(general)</small>
: You move more quickly on foot. Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Lie to Me" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Lie to Me''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You can use Deception to weave traps to trip up anyone trying to deceive you. If you can engage in conversation with someone trying to Lie to you, use your Deception DC if it is higher than your Perception DC to determine whether they succeed. This doesn't apply if you don't have a back-and-forth dialogue, such as when someone attempts to Lie during a long speech.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Loose Cannon" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Loose Cannon''' <small>(archetype)</small>
: Your unpredictable gunfire often catches enemies off guard or hits unprotected areas. Make a ranged Strike with a firearm against a creature that you haven't already attacked on this turn. You gain a circumstance bonus to damage on this Strike equal to the number of weapon damage dice. The Strike gains the following failure effect.

'''Failure''' The firearm misfires, but it doesn't cause the other critical failure effects listed below.

'''Critical Failure''' The firearm misfires and also explodes. It becomes Broken, and it deals its normal weapon damage to all creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:20] centered on the firearm, with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] against your class DC. This damage includes any from the weapon's fundamental and property runes.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Natural Ambition" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Natural Ambition''' <small>(human)</small>
: You were raised to be ambitious and always reach for the stars, leading you to progress quickly in your chosen field. You gain a 1st-level class feat for your class. You must meet the prerequisites, but you can select the feat later in the character creation process in order to determine which prerequisites you meet.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/creatures/eyes/human-single-blue.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Perception Expertise" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Perception Expertise''' <small>(alchemist, animist, champion, cleric, druid, exemplar, kineticist, monk, sorcerer, witch, wizard)</small>
: You remain alert to threats around you. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to expert.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/symbols/question-stone-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Second Doctrine" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Second Doctrine''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: You gain the second benefit of your doctrine:

* Cloistered Cleric

* Warpriest
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-helmet-metal.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Second Doctrine (Warpriest)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Second Doctrine (Warpriest)''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: You're trained in martial weapons.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Shield Block" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Shield Block''' <small>(general)</small>
: '''Trigger''' While you have your shield raised, you would take physical damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) from an attack.

You snap your shield in place to ward off a blow. Your shield prevents you from taking an amount of damage up to the shield's Hardness. You and the shield each take any remaining damage, possibly breaking or destroying the shield.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Titan Wrestler" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Titan Wrestler''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You can attempt to Disarm, Grapple, Reposition, Shove, or Trip creatures up to two sizes larger than you, or up to three sizes larger than you if you're legendary in Athletics.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Untrained Improvisation" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Untrained Improvisation''' <small>(general)</small>
: You've learned how to handle situations when you're out of your depth. Your proficiency bonus to untrained skill checks is equal to your level –2. This improves to your level –1 at 5th level and your full level at 7th level. This doesn't allow you to use the skill's trained actions.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Ward Medic" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Ward Medic''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You've studied in large medical wards, treating several patients at once and tending to all their needs. When you use Treat Disease or Treat Wounds, you can treat up to two targets. If you're a master in Medicine, you can treat up to four targets, and if you're legendary, you can treat up to eight targets.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-helmet-metal.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Warpriest" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Warpriest''' <small>(cleric)</small>
: You have trained in the more militant doctrine of your church, focusing on both spells and battle.

'''First Doctrine (1st):''' You're trained in light and medium armor, and you have expert proficiency in Fortitude saves. You gain the Shield Block general feat, a reaction to reduce damage with a shield. If your deity's favored weapon is a simple weapon or an unarmed attack, you gain the Deadly Simplicity cleric feat. At 13th level, if you gain the Divine Defense class feature, you also gain expert proficiency in light and medium armor.

'''Second Doctrine (3rd):''' You're trained in martial weapons.

'''Third Doctrine (7th):''' You gain expert proficiency with your deity's favored weapon, martial weapons, simple weapons, and unarmed attacks. When you critically succeed at an attack roll using your deity's favored weapon, you apply the weapon's critical specialization effect; you can use your spell DC in place of your class DC.

'''Fourth Doctrine (11th):''' Your proficiency ranks for the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics increase to expert.

'''Fifth Doctrine (15th):''' Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to master. When you roll a success at a Fortitude save, you get a critical success instead.

'''Final Doctrine (19th):''' You gain master proficiency with your deity's favored weapon, spell attack modifier, and spell DC.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Wrestler Dedication" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Wrestler Dedication''' <small>(archetype, dedication)</small>
: Your training in the wrestling arts has made you particularly adept at moving, striking, and grappling while unencumbered. You become an expert in Athletics and gain the Titan Wrestler skill feat. You don't take the -2 circumstance penalty for making a lethal attack with your nonlethal unarmed attacks. In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC when resisting an opponent's attempts to Grapple you or Swallow you Whole.

Wrestler

=====Actions=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Avoid Notice" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Avoid Notice''' <small>(exploration)</small>
: You attempt a Stealth check to avoid notice while traveling at half speed. If you're Avoiding Notice at the start of an encounter, you usually roll a Stealth check instead of a Perception check both to determine your initiative and to see if the enemies notice you (based on their Perception DCs, as normal for Sneak, regardless of their initiative check results).
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Blend In" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Blend In''' <small>(exploration)</small>
: '''Overcome''' @Check[deception|dc:35], @Check[society|dc:37], @Check[stealth|dc:35]

Sometimes the best option for crossing a crowded party is to act natural and join the crowd.

If a PC successfully Blends In, roll a secret @Check[perception|dc:34|traits:secret] for them to notice that something seems off about one of the guests. On a success, inform them about the Dispel a Disguise Opportunity.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Defend" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Defend''' <small>(exploration)</small>
: You move at half your travel speed with your shield raised. If combat breaks out, you gain the benefits of Raising a Shield before your first turn begins.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Raise a Shield" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Raise a Shield'''
: '''Requirements''' You are wielding a shield.

You position your shield to protect yourself. When you have Raised a Shield, you gain its listed circumstance bonus to AC. Your shield remains raised until the start of your next turn.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Raise a Shield" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Raise a Shield'''
: '''Requirements''' You are wielding a shield.

You position your shield to protect yourself. When you have Raised a Shield, you gain its listed circumstance bonus to AC. Your shield remains raised until the start of your next turn.

</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎒 Inventory</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Weapons=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/hand/gauntlet-simple-leather-brown-gold.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="+1 Striking Handwraps of Mighty Blows" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[+1 Striking Handwraps of Mighty Blows]] || 1 || 0 || 2 || 35 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/black-powder-knuckle-dusters.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Black Powder Knuckle Dusters" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Black Powder Knuckle Dusters]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 8 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/thrown/bolas-stone.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Bola" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Bola]] || 4 || 0.1 || 0 || 5 sp
|}

=====Armor & Shields=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-gorget-steel-white.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Half Plate" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Half Plate]] || 1 || 3 || 1 || 18 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/shields/steel-shield.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Steel Shield" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Steel Shield]] || 1 || 1 || 0 || 2 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/shields/specific-shields/sturdy-shield.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Sturdy Shield (Minor)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Sturdy Shield (Minor)]] || 1 || 1 || 4 || 100 gp
|}

=====Consumables=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/alchemical-items/alchemical-elixirs/elixir-of-life.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Elixir of Life (Minor)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Elixir of Life (Minor)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 3 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/consumables/potions/bottle-round-corked-orante-red.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Healing Potion (Lesser)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Healing Potion (Lesser)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 12 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Alarm (Rank 1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Alarm (Rank 1)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Cleanse Affliction (Rank 2)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Cleanse Affliction (Rank 2)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 12 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Dispel Magic (Rank 2)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Dispel Magic (Rank 2)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 12 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Helpful Steps (Rank 1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Helpful Steps (Rank 1)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Lock (Rank 1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Lock (Rank 1)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/scrolls/scroll-symbol-eye-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Scroll of Mending (Rank 1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Scroll of Mending (Rank 1)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/talismans/snapleaf.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Snapleaf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Snapleaf]] || 1 || 0 || 3 || 9 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/alchemical-items/alchemical-tools/universal-solvent.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Universal Solvent (Moderate)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Universal Solvent (Moderate)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 5 || 21 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/wands/magic-wands/magic-wand.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Wand of Concordant Choir (Rank 1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Wand of Concordant Choir (Rank 1)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 60 gp
|}

=====Equipment=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/materials/cold-Iron-chunk.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Cold Iron Chunk" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Cold Iron Chunk]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 10 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/containers/bags/pouch-gold-green.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Healer's Toolkit" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Healer's Toolkit]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 5 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/waist/belt-leather-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Lifting Belt" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Lifting Belt]] || 1 || 0.1 || 4 || 80 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/neck/handkerchief-bandana-blue.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Masquerade Scarf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Masquerade Scarf]] || 1 || 0 || 2 || 30 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/tools/smithing/anvil.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Repair Toolkit" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Repair Toolkit]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 2 gp
|}

=====Bags & Storage=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/containers/bags/pack-leather-white-tan.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Backpack" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Backpack]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 sp
|-
| colspan="6" |
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/survival/rope-wrapped-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Rope" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Rope
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/rations.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Rations" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Rations ×2
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/waterskin.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Waterskin" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Waterskin
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/survival/soap.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Soap" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Soap
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/climbing-kit.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Climbing Kit" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Climbing Kit
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/containers/ammunition/quiver-simple-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Bedroll" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Bedroll
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/stone/geode-raw-tan.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Flint and Steel" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Flint and Steel
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/lights/torch-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Torch" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Torch ×5
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/chalk.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Chalk" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Chalk ×10
|}

=====Treasure & Currency=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/copper-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Copper Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Copper Pieces]] || 4 || 1 || 0 || 1 cp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/gold-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Gold Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Gold Pieces]] || 16 || 1 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/platinum-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Platinum Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Platinum Pieces]] || 1 || 1 || 0 || 1 pp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/silver-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Silver Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Silver Pieces]] || 4 || 1 || 0 || 1 sp
|}

</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🔮 Spellcasting</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Divine Focus Spells (Divine) — Focus=====

'''Rank 1'''
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/control/buff-strength-muscle-damage.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Athletic Rush" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Athletic Rush]] <small>[cleric, focus, manipulate]</small>

=====Divine Prepared Spells (Divine) — Prepared=====

'''Rank 1''' <small>(0/3 slots)</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/sonic/explosion-shock-sound-wave.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Concordant Choir" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Concordant Choir]] <small>[concentrate, sonic]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/detect-magic.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Detect Magic" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Detect Magic]] <small>[cantrip, concentrate, detection, manipulate]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/ray-of-enfeeblement.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Enfeeble" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Enfeeble]] <small>[concentrate, manipulate]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/harm.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Harm" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Harm]] <small>[manipulate, void]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/light/projectile-needles-salvo-white.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Needle Darts" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Needle Darts]] <small>[attack, cantrip, concentrate, manipulate, metal]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/protection.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Protection" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Protection]] <small>[concentrate, manipulate]</small>

'''Rank 2''' <small>(0/3 slots)</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/blood-vendetta.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Blood Vendetta" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Blood Vendetta]] <small>[concentrate, curse]</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/acid/dissolve-bone-white.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Sudden Blight" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Sudden Blight]] <small>[concentrate, manipulate, void]</small>

'''Rank 3''' <small>(0/7 slots)</small>
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/vampiric-touch.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Vampiric Feast" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Vampiric Feast]] <small>[concentrate, death, manipulate, void]</small>

=====Other Spells=====
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/bane.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Bane" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Bane]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/light/explosion-glow-spiral-teal.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Benediction" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Benediction]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/bless.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Bless" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Bless]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/command.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Command" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Command]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/divine-lance.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Divine Lance" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Divine Lance]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/fear.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Fear" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Fear]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/guidance.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Guidance" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Guidance]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/life/cross-worn-green.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Heal" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Heal]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/helpful-steps.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Helpful Steps" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Helpful Steps]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/light.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Light" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Light]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/create-water.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Rousing Splash" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Rousing Splash]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/creatures/abilities/wolf-heads-swirl-purple.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Calm" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Calm]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/consumables/fruit/berry-clustered-white.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Cleanse Affliction" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Cleanse Affliction]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/detect-alignment.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Clear Mind" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Clear Mind]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/dispel-magic.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Dispel Magic" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Dispel Magic]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/fire/beam-jet-stream-embers.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Inner Radiance Torrent" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Inner Radiance Torrent]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/glitterdust.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Revealing Light" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Revealing Light]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/see-invisibility.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="See the Unseen" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[See the Unseen]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/wholeness-of-body.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Sound Body" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Sound Body]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/melee/strike-sword-slashing-red.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Spiritual Armament" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Spiritual Armament]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/etheral-jaunt.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Sure Footing" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Sure Footing]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/consumables/fruit/apple-orange-glass.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Thermal Remedy" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Thermal Remedy]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/comprehend-language.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Translate" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Translate]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/wounds/injury-eyes-blood-red.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Blindness" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Blindness]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/holy/prayer-hands-glowing-yellow-white.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Crisis of Faith" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Crisis of Faith]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/darkvision.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Deep Sight" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Deep Sight]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/haste.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Haste" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Haste]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/life/ankh-gold-blue.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Heroism" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Heroism]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/life-pact.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Life Pact" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Life Pact]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/martyrs-intervention.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Martyr's Intervention" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Martyr's Intervention]]
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/spells/roaring-applause.webp" width="18" height="18" alt="Roaring Applause" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> [[Roaring Applause]]

</div>
</div>

''Last synced: 2026-06-20 01:33''
[[Category:Characters]]
